# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0025 - QUIC CRT client resumption-ticket snapshot and early-data gate architecture

## Purpose

Describe the smallest honest 0-RTT prerequisite: opaque resumption-ticket ownership on the managed client/runtime path plus an explicit early-data gate that stays closed.

## Scope

This slice covers only opaque resumption-ticket ownership and an explicit early-data gate. It does not add 0-RTT packet processing, PSK derivation, replay handoff, or a public API expansion.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0127

## Design Summary

The current managed runtime already has the client handshake and internal ticket-ingress seams, but it does not own a resumption-ticket snapshot. This slice adds a runtime-owned opaque ticket carrier at the client host/runtime boundary, copies the first accepted ticket bytes from the existing bridge ingress seam into that carrier, and keeps early-data admission explicitly closed. No PSK derivation, replay handoff, 0-RTT packet path, or anti-replay story is added here.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicClientConnectionHost.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntimeEventModels.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicPostHandshakeTicketTestSupport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0127.cs

## Risks

- If ticket state is modeled as structured PSK or replay metadata, the slice will overgrow into broad session-management infrastructure that the repository has not proven.
- If the early-data gate is loosened before or after the snapshot exists, the repo will claim 0-RTT support it cannot honestly provide.
- If the gate changes the supported public boundary, the slice will widen beyond the current proof floor.
